# GnuCash Import — Usage Guide

*Mode: direct · 🔌 offline (no LLM)*

Part III of the KR Choksey → GnuCash flow. Converts the Part II Bills workbook into importable GnuCash multi-split CSVs — Purchase, SLBM and Sale — using FIFO cost basis and the long-term holding threshold from your .gnucash book and an editable config in Data/settings/. Runs fully offline.

## When to use it

Run this last of the three KRChoksey tabs, after Reconcile (Part II). It produces the CSVs you import into GnuCash.

## Inputs

- **Part II Bills workbook** (required) — accepts: Excel (.xlsx) matching *-KRC-Bills-Recon.xlsx.
  - The Part II Bills workbook — pick it from the list of prior outputs.
  - ⚠️ Run Part II first; the picker only lists Bills workbooks from earlier runs.
- **GnuCash book (.gnucash)** (required) — accepts: GnuCash file (.gnucash).
  - Your GnuCash book — used for account paths and FIFO cost basis.
  - ⚠️ Close the book in GnuCash before running. It is read only, never modified.

## How to run

1. Pick the Part II Bills workbook from the dropdown.
2. Select your .gnucash book (closed in GnuCash).
3. Click Run, then open the output folder.
4. Import each CSV into GnuCash (File > Import > Import Transactions from CSV).

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-KRC-GnuCash/
```

Files produced:

- **`Purchase.csv`** — One transaction per purchase: Dr the security account (with share quantity), Cr the KR Choksey broker account, for the net/bill amount.
- **`SLBM.csv`** — One transaction per SLBM bill: Dr KR Choksey broker, Cr Income from SLBS (net lending proceeds, offered to tax as income).
- **`Sale.csv`** — Multi-split per sale: Dr broker (proceeds), Cr security at FIFO cost basis (negative shares), Cr Long/Short Term Capital Gain (a loss appears as a debit).
- **`Review.csv`** — Only created when something needs attention — an unmatched security name, or a sale with insufficient FIFO lots.

## Tips

Every transaction's Amount column sums to zero, so each imports as a balanced GnuCash transaction. The long-term threshold and account paths come from your book plus the editable config in Data/settings/.

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| A Review.csv appears. | Open it: either a security name didn't match an account (add/rename it) or a sale lacked enough FIFO lots (check earlier purchases were imported). |
| Capital gain looks wrong. | Confirm the holding-period threshold and account paths in Data/settings/ match your setup. |
